case.label: Jordan M. accommodation snapshot (demo seed)
notes: Hackathon demo seed image used by the live web flow when the user taps the Jordan M. preview.
Expectations match the pre-reviewed text the app previously hard-coded for this image so the eval can measure how close live Gemma 4 extraction lands on the demo path.
Accommodations and modifications appear in separate sections; the eval should keep them distinguishable.

checks.details: Accommodation extraction should now return plain text, not JSON.

cases.output:
Student Information
Jordan M.
Grade: 7
Date: Sample / Synthetic
Primary Area of Need: Auditory Processing Disorder

Student Accommodation & Modification Snapshot

LEARNING PROFILE

CLASSROOM ACCOMMODATIONS
1. Provide written and verbal directions.
2. Break directions and assignments into smaller steps.
3. Allow extended time to process information
4. Provide written or digital copies of key information
5. Seat near the teacher and away from noise or high-traffic areas.
6. Use visual supports such as graphic organizers, charts, timelines, and anchor charts.
7. Allow use of assistive technology (text-to-speech, speech-to-text, audiobooks, dictation).

PREFERENCES THAT SUPPORT INDEPENDENCE
Jordan prefers accommodations that allow them to work independently.
Avoid calling attention to processing or reading differences.
Encouragement and specific feedback help build confidence.

NOTES FOR TEACHERS
Purpose: This plan outlines the accommodations and modifications designed to help ensure Jordan has equal access to grade-level curriculum by reducing the impact of processing difficulties and supporting comprehension, expression, and independent performance.

COMMUNICATION
Visual reminders and charts are available
in the classroom.

WHEN ACCOMMODATIONS ARE MOST IMPORTANT
• Please reach out with questions or concerns.
• Consistent, positive communication helps us support Jordan's success.
cases.raw_content:
Student Information
Jordan M.
Grade: 7
Date: Sample / Synthetic
Primary Area of Need: Auditory Processing Disorder

Student Accommodation & Modification Snapshot

LEARNING PROFILE

CLASSROOM ACCOMMODATIONS
1. Provide written and verbal directions.
2. Break directions and assignments into smaller steps.
3. Allow extended time to process information
4. Provide written or digital copies of key information
5. Seat near the teacher and away from noise or high-traffic areas.
6. Use visual supports such as graphic organizers, charts, timelines, and anchor charts.
7. Allow use of assistive technology (text-to-speech, speech-to-text, audiobooks, dictation).

PREFERENCES THAT SUPPORT INDEPENDENCE
Jordan prefers accommodations that allow them to work independently.
Avoid calling attention to processing or reading differences.
Encouragement and specific feedback help build confidence.

NOTES FOR TEACHERS
Purpose: This plan outlines the accommodations and modifications designed to help ensure Jordan has equal access to grade-level curriculum by reducing the impact of processing difficulties and supporting comprehension, expression, and independent performance.

COMMUNICATION
Visual reminders and charts are available
in the classroom.

WHEN ACCOMMODATIONS ARE MOST IMPORTANT
• Please reach out with questions or concerns.
• Consistent, positive communication helps us support Jordan's success.
